decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Since the heat spreader conductivity is already at its maximum bound (500.0), I will increase the base plate conductivity to improve vertical heat extraction toward the cooled boundaries. This is a supporting lever that remains within bounds and avoids the geometry legality issues seen in previous runs.

changes.reason: Doubling the base plate conductivity enhances heat flow from the chip/spreader assembly to the left/right Dirichlet boundaries, helping to lower the peak temperature without altering geometry.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
Improved thermal gradient across the base plate
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck is primarily lateral spreading rather than vertical extraction
Base material conductivity increase has less impact per unit than spreader conductivity, so multiple iterations may be needed
